text:
prescriptions, the dispensing of
medications or communication with physicians and
care teams (59,61). Tools such as the Transition
Readiness Assessment Questionnaire (TRAQ) or the Transition
Care Index allow assessment and improvement of this preparation. Pediatric care is distinguished by attention
to growth, puberty and nutrition, whereas
adult care includes issues of fertility, pregnancy,
cancer surveillance and management of
comorbidities (59).
The barriers identified to a successful transition are multiple: attachment to the pediatrician and fear of abandonment, low
self-esteem, lack of self-management skills,
organizational difficulties and, in some contexts,
economic barriers. The timing of transfer should remain
flexible, ideally during a period of clinical remission, in order to
limit the risk of loss of follow-up. Parents should
be supported in the process to encourage their child's increasing
autonomy, while a transition coordinator, if present, facilitates continuity and prevents loss of
contact with the medical teams
